strict.rationale_for_score: The summary contains a severe hallucination: it attributes the quote 'I didn't know what I was about to see' to the tour manager, whereas the source text explicitly states this was said by Michael Barnett. Additionally, the summary incorrectly states that 'the bus is set to perform,' when the article says the band (Lady Antebellum) is performing; a bus cannot perform at an awards show. The grammar is also poor with run-on sentences and lack of proper punctuation.

lenient.rationale_for_score: The summary contains a severe hallucination regarding the tour manager; the text states Michael Barnett (the videographer) didn't know what to expect, but the summary incorrectly attributes this quote to the tour manager. Additionally, it falsely claims the bus is 'set to perform' at the awards show, whereas the article states the band (not the bus) is set to perform. These are clear inconsistencies with the source text.

lenient.adjustment_notes: I agree with the strict judge's assessment. The summary contains a critical factual error (hallucination) by attributing Michael Barnett's quote to the tour manager, which violates consistency. It also makes a logical error stating 'the bus is set to perform,' whereas the band performs, affecting coherence and relevance. The lack of punctuation and run-on sentences further degrade fluency. No leniency is warranted for these fundamental flaws.

These are severe consistency failures. The fluency score of 3 is also appropriate given the run-on sentence structure and lack of proper punctuation/grammar in the summary.
